codeReview.summary: I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, `backend-worker` guidance, commit `f5b6262c381ab20ff3d99567af316c053464b8bf`, and the current `JournalEntryService`, `DealerReceiptService`, `TenantRuntimeEnforcementService`, `TenantRuntimeAccessService`, and related test state. The implementation narrows the touched `JournalEntryService` log lines to operational-only messages, the current branch state shows `@Override` annotations on the relevant `JournalEntryService` and `DealerReceiptService` overrides, `TenantRuntimeEnforcementService` now guards null tenant-state/reason/audit metadata via shared fallback helpers, and both runtime failure helpers now declare `serialVersionUID` with focused regression coverage in the tenant runtime test suites. I did not find a high-confidence correctness, regression, or contract-adherence defect within the reviewed feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared command manifest does not expose an `-Xlint:all` compile recipe, so workers still have to hand-roll a bespoke Maven command whenever a feature requires explicit override-warning proof.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml:6-9` only defines plain `compile`/`typecheck`; the feature handoff `/home/realnigga/.factory/missions/cf35a73d-f4bd-4cf3-9488-fee34cf89e18/handoffs/2026-04-05T18-03-16-658Z__foundation-pr183-security-fixes__0762753e-8336-4955-ac52-efec75bb89de.json:26` records the ad-hoc command `cd "/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/erp-domain" && MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn clean -Dmaven.compiler.showWarnings=true -Dmaven.compiler.compilerArgs='-Xlint:all' -DskipTests compile`.

summary: PASS. I found the feature implementation aligned with scope: sensitive journal retry/idempotency logs are sanitized, the tenant-runtime null/serialization hardening is covered by focused tests, and I did not find a high-confidence feature-scope defect. I noted one shared-state gap: `.factory/services.yaml` still lacks a reusable `-Xlint:all` compile command.
